key_insight: A continuous conditional diffusion model can generate realistic microstructures from process parameters with much less training data by using vicinal-loss regularization for sparse continuous conditions.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster process-structure surrogate modeling for materials design and optimization, reducing reliance on expensive physics-based simulations for high-throughput exploration.
method: Train a denoising diffusion model on paired process-microstructure data
Condition generation on continuous processing parameters with classifier-free guidance
Use vicinal-loss training and DDIM sampling to improve performance in data-sparse regions
evidence_signal: The model was trained on a compact dataset and was able to generate low-carbon steel microstructures conditioned on manganese composition that matched key morphology, grain size, phase fraction, and interfacial area distributions.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and coverage of the limited training dataset, especially when extrapolating to poorly sampled process conditions.
paper_title: Data-efficient continuous conditional denoising diffusion model for microstructure generation
